sourceMovie.title: Dragonkeeper
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Dragonkeeper (2024) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Raya and the Last Dragon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dragonkeeper clicked for you, Raya and the Last Dragon (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and family, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dragonkeeper?
faq.answer: Try Raya and the Last Dragon, Trollhunters: Rise of the Titans, Wish Dragon—each shares DNA with Dragonkeeper while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dragonkeeper?
faq.answer: Dragonkeeper (2024) is best described as Animation, Family, Action, Adventure, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dragonkeeper worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
